patterns.name: Google API Key
patterns.description: Google Cloud API key for accessing Google services
patterns.risk: Access to enabled Google APIs. Can incur charges, access data, or abuse quotas.
patterns.fix: Use GOOGLE_API_KEY environment variable. Restrict key to specific APIs and referrers in the GCP console.

patterns.name: Google OAuth Client Secret
patterns.risk: Can be used to impersonate your application and access user data.
patterns.fix: Store in environment variable or secret manager. Never commit client_secret.json.

patterns.name: GCP Service Account Key
patterns.description: Google Cloud service account JSON key file contents
patterns.risk: Full access to GCP resources granted to the service account. Often has broad permissions.
patterns.fix: Use Workload Identity Federation instead of key files. If keys are required, mount via secret manager.

patterns.name: Firebase Configuration with Sensitive Fields
patterns.description: Firebase configuration object (note: Firebase API keys are safe to expose in client-side code, but the config may include other sensitive values)
patterns.risk: Low risk for API key alone, but config may include sensitive project details.
patterns.fix: Firebase client config is designed to be public, but ensure Firestore/RTDB security rules are properly configured.
